rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical synthesis of cyclopropanes (including spirocyclopropanes) from C-H acids and alkylidenemalononitriles using pyridinium bromide as a redox mediator in an undivided cell.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of bromide to Br2 (mediator regeneration); cathodic reduction of pyridinium cation to pyridine (base generator) with concomitant H2 evolution.

key_intermediates.name: N-substituted pyridinium salt (alpha-pyridinio C-H acid, pre-ylide)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes 2 Br- to Br2 while the cathode reduces 2 PyH+ to 2 Py + H2; Py deprotonates the C-H acid whose anion is brominated by Br2 to give A; a second equivalent of Py displaces bromide forming pyridinium ylide B, which undergoes Michael addition to the alkylidenemalononitrile followed by intramolecular SN2 displacement of pyridine to close the cyclopropane 3.
notes: Mechanism is proposed by analogy (last cyclization step postulated following Abaszadeh's work on pyridinium ylide + benzylidenemalononitrile cyclopropanation). Authors note NaBr in MeOH is a more effective mediator than PyHBr in MeCN, attributed to mechanistic particularities but not probed experimentally. Bromine formation at the anode is supported by visual observation of its color; H2 evolution at the cathode is observed especially under unstirred conditions.
